sourceMovie.title: Office Space
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Office Space (1999) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Mike Judge's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Night at the Museum
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Office Space clicked for you, Night at the Museum (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Office Space?
faq.answer: Try Night at the Museum, A Bug's Life, Horrible Bosses—each shares DNA with Office Space while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Office Space?
faq.answer: Office Space (1999) is best described as Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Office Space worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
